programme.title: FROM
programme.desc: Julie finds comfort in her new friends. Jim and Tabitha deal with their struggling marriage. Ethan ventures off into the forest with Victor. Father Khatri urges Boyd to embrace his role as leader. Sara receives another horrifying directive.

programme.title: FROM
programme.desc: Jim, Tabitha and Ethan begin asking questions about where they are in the hopes it may lead them home. Ellis and Fatima show Julie the lighter side of life in town. Jade struggles to make sense of their situation. Boyd seeks guidance.

programme.title: FROM
programme.desc: Boyd’s plan to head off into the forest provokes an unexpected response from Kenny. Jade enlists Jim’s help with an experiment. Tabitha makes a chilling discovery that sends her down a unique path searching for answers.

programme.title: FROM
programme.desc: Father Khatri tells Boyd why he believes Sara may be useful. Jim and Tabitha find comfort in each other. Colony House celebrates the one-year anniversary of Fatima’s arrival with a party that goes terribly wrong.

programme.title: FROM
programme.desc: In the wake of another death, Boyd questions his decision to leave. Kenny steps up and embraces his new role. Jim rallies the town around his idea to build a radio tower. Fatima urges Ellis to make amends with his father before it’s too late.

programme.title: FROM
programme.desc: Boyd and Sara journey deeper into the forest than Boyd has ever gone, encountering new and terrifying mysteries. Jim’s confidence in the radio tower begins to wane. An unexpected tragedy causes Donna to unravel. Kenny and Kristi share a moment.

programme.title: FROM
programme.desc: Boyd draws strength from an unlikely source. Jim’s radio tower yields consequences which rock him to his very core. The hole that Tabitha has been digging leads her somewhere, and to someone, she could never have expected.

programme.title: FROM
programme.desc: In Sheriff Boyd’s absence, Donna and Kenny struggle to manage the chaos as a busload of unwitting newcomers arrives in town. Victor and Tabitha go on a chilling journey through the nightmarish labyrinth of tunnels beneath the town.

programme.title: FROM
programme.desc: Some of the newcomers accept their fate, some don't, and some die. Jim spends a night in the collapsed house with Tom and a dying newcomer, while Boyd shares a conversation with Martin. Tabitha and Victor make their way to Victor's hiding spot.

programme.title: FROM
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the bus arrival, Boyd returns and takes charge, while Ellis and Fatima make plans for the future. Kenny and Ellis talk to a girl from the bus who has something on her mind, while Jade talks to Victor about something on his mind.

programme.title: FROM
programme.desc: Jim has a new theory about what's going on with the town. Donna lays down the rules of Colony House to Randall. Ethan tries to make up with Victor, and Kenny finds out what Boyd has been hiding from him. Meanwhile, Boyd has an unexpected conversation.

programme.title: FROM
programme.desc: Sara goes about the town talking to the people whose her earlier actions she hurt, and Ethan confronts her. Jade tries to find out more from Victor about the man in the photos and has to play a lullaby on the violin, while Tabitha reaches out to the children in her visions.

programme.title: FROM
programme.desc: Donna is forced to secretly start rationing at Colony House, but isn't a secret for very long. The worms inside Boyd grow stronger, and he is forced to find a way to get rid of them when Ellis is badly wounded and needs a blood transfusion.

programme.title: FROM
programme.desc: Boyd's ballerina nightmare somehow spreads to Mari and Elgin. Kristi conducts an autopsy on the dead monster to try and find a weakness the townspeople can exploit against the other monsters. Fatima gets unexpected medical news, while Kenny reveals a secret to Kristi.

programme.title: FROM
programme.desc: Jim and Randall suspect there's more to the town secrets than meet the eye and Randall's paranoia takes a dangerous turn. Tabitha prods Victor into remembering more about his past. Strange dreams plague the town in the wake of the monster's body.

programme.title: FROM
programme.desc: The cicadas are flying, and no one can sleep safely, day or night. Things at Colony House grow tense, while Boyd, Jim, Donna, and Randall spend a night in the RV when they can't get back to town before night falls and the monsters come out.

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
programme.desc: Harry Potter lives his second year at Hogwarts with Ron and Hermione when a message on the wall announces that the legendary Chamber of Secrets has been opened. The trio soon realize that, to save the school, it will take a lot of courage.

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban
programme.desc: Harry Potter, Ron and Hermione return to Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry for their third year of study, where they delve into the mystery surrounding an escaped prisoner who poses a dangerous threat to the young wizard.

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire
programme.desc: When Harry Potter's name emerges from the Goblet of Fire, he becomes a competitor in a grueling battle for glory among three wizarding schools—the Triwizard Tournament. But since Harry never submitted his name for the Tournament, who did? Now Harry must confront a deadly dragon, fierce water demons and an enchanted maze only to find himself in the cruel grasp of He Who Must Not Be Named.

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix
programme.desc: Returning for his fifth year of study at Hogwarts, Harry is stunned to find that his warnings about the return of Lord Voldemort have been ignored. Left with no choice, Harry takes matters into his own hands, training a small group of students to defend themselves against the dark arts.

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince
programme.desc: During Harry Potter's sixth year at Hogwarts, he finds a book that once belonged to the mysterious Half-Blood Prince that earns him the respect of his new Potions professor Horace Slughorn. In addition, Dumbledore must prepare Harry for the ultimate final confrontation by finding out the secret behind Voldemort's power. Meanwhile, a hidden enemy waits in the shadows to carry out a mission given to him by the Dark Lord.

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 1
programme.desc: Harry Potter is tasked with the dangerous and seemingly impossible task of locating and destroying Voldemort's remaining Horcruxes. Harry must rely on Ron and Hermione more than ever, but dark forces threaten to tear them apart.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Marie stays at Raymond and Debra's house after she gets in a fight with Frank. Meanwhile, Frank has the time of his life by himself at home, and Raymond starts getting used to having his mom spoil him again.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray's upper class in-laws come for a visit, he begs his parents to be nice to them. Ray admits to his parents that he doesn't always see eye-to-eye with Debra's folks but manages to fake it, and he'd like his parents to do the same when they all go out and try to have a family dinner.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray gets tired of being treated like a kid at his father's poker game, so he decides to ignore his father's advice and ends up losing all his money.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Although Marie has always cooked the traditional turkey dinner for Thanksgiving, Debra decides it's her turn to prepare the holiday meal, but not wanting to compete with Marie's turkey, she chooses to serve fish.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray's free time goes to coaching Nemo's basketball team in preparation for the Pizza League championships, he is reminded by Debra that their kids are growing up fast and he's missing precious time with them.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray discovers that the autographed Mickey Mantle ball he got as a kid from his father isn't authentic. Feeling hurt by the fact that his father wasn't truthful about the ball, Ray decides he's going to tell Ally the truth about Santa Claus.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Raymond misses an interview with Terry Bradshaw about ghostwriting his book because he has to take Ally and Michael to the pediatrician. Debra is sick at home, and Marie comes over to take care of her.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray can't face the fact that everybody thinks that his brother Robert is "classically handsome," while they consider him "adorable like a hamster."

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra, already annoyed at getting stuck driving the old car Ray bought from his parents, really gets upset when she discovers that it was his old "make-out-mobile."

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray finds out that the diamond on Debra's engagement ring is a fake, so he steals it from her to get it changed, only to find out after that she already had it exchanged it with her grandma's $15,000 diamond.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray and his family play a game of Scruples after their cable goes out, they find themselves arguing about honesty. To make matters worse, when Ray complains to the cable company about the disruption in service, he is informed that he has an illegal hookup.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Debra tries to change Ray's pessimistic attitude about winning the Sports Writer of the Year Award, he agrees to change his ways and become more positive, but he soon has to suffer the downside of being the only optimist in his family.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray brings home a stray bulldog that reminds him of the dog he had to give up as a boy due to his allergies, but his brother Robert bonds with the dog and the siblings have to decide who gets to keep him.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: The neighbourhood is up in arms over Ray's noisy dad and meddling mom, and the neighbours demand a meeting at Ray's and Debra's house.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: A popular radio psychologist visits the Barone household to interview Debra for a book on the American family. But the doctor finds that the rest of the eccentric Barones are more captivating.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: In this flashback episode, Ray and Debra remember their days in their first apartment.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is devastated when he discovers his family lied to him about his television debut on a sports talk show. When he gets a second chance to appear on the same show, his family decides it's time they gave him some pointers on how to correct his awkwardness with certain words.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Marie and Frank's stubbornness goes to far, Ray gets to use the "active technique" he and Debra learned in parenting class on his parents.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: At the urging of his parents and Debra, Raymond reluctantly takes Robert for a night out on the anniversary of his brother's divorce.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ally becomes bored of the piano after three lessons by Marie, Ray decides to give the former a lesson about not quitting, as he doesn't want her to lose interest so quickly. He asks Marie to teach him the piano again.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray suffers an anxiety attack while playing golf, after he tricked Debra into letting him go. Believing that he is sick, Ray goes to see a doctor who tells him that he is properly suffering from stress and that he needs to find a way to relax, such as playing more golf.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: At a big surprise party for Frank and Marie's 40th. wedding anniversary, Ray is shocked to learn that his parents were once separated for a year and that they might have gotten divorced, if Frank hadn't returned because Ray broke his arm.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: After Debra reads a newspaper article about people who enjoy being at work more than being at home, she convinces Ray to set up a home office.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Sick of reading Ally the same stories night after night, Debra decides to try her hand at writing a children's book.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray, feeling guilty about only getting his dad a card for his 65th. birthday, makes up for it by buying him a fancy and expensive aquarium just like the one he always admired in the Chinese restaurant. Frank, at first adores the gift, but after one of the fish dies, he starts hating it.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray makes a startling discovery when he takes Debra to his 20th. high school reunion. It turns out that Debra's "one of them," them being the cool people who hated him and used to push him into the girls' bathroom.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Marie crashes and ruins her Tupperware party, Debra decides that she's had just about enough of her mother-in-law's constant interference. She puts pen to paper and writes a letter to her mother-in-law.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: All Ray wants for Christmas is a little loving from his wife and he's willing to try anything to have his holiday wish come true.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Feeling left out when he thinks that his father selectively asked his brother to join a Civil War re-enactment, Ray voluntarily enlists for the Battle of Gettysburg. As the men prepare for Battle, a funny thing happens, Ray is finally able to tell Frank that he wishes they did more together.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ally has to do a school project on genealogy and interview her oldest living relative, Ray helps her track down his father Frank's long-lost Aunt Sarina in Italy, and they decide to write her a letter. Things get interesting when Aunt Sarina takes the letter and the "stop by if you if you're ever in America" literally.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Raymond chooses his mother's spaghetti and meatballs over his wife's lemon chicken for dinner, Debra blows a gasket. With good intentions, Ray asks his mom to teach Debra how to make her world-famous dish.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Raymond questions Debra about the budget and says he can do a better job - so she lets him try. In a few weeks, Ray bounces checks and has $300 in overdraft fees. He creates a fake checkbook so it all looks good on paper.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Raymond decides to accompany his brother Robert, who is a policeman, on a ride-along. Initially, spending night shift in the back of Robert's patrol car bores Raymond, but his boredom quickly turns to terror during a chance visit to Nemo's restaurant.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ally has been sleeping in Ray and Debra's bed a lot recently, and Ray is tired of it because he can't get a good night's sleep. The whole family has their own opinions on the issue. Ally complains that there are monsters in her room. Debra was sleeping in the room with her, but Ray didn't want her to do that. So, Ray gets his mother to spend the night in Ally's room with her...without Debra knowing about it.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Major Kawalsky is possessed by a Goa'uld, and the SGC must find a way to remove it without killing him.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: An alien civilization is forced to reconsider their views on women when Carter rebels against their social customs.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Members of SG-1 become infected with an alien virus that turns them into primitive beings. Dr. Fraiser must find a cure to save the team and the alien population from whom it was contracted.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: The SG-1 team is sent after the SG-9 team that has failed to return. They find that SG-9's captain, having been treated like a god by planetary inhabitants, is drunk with power and is tyrannizing them. Can they overcome him?

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: When Jack is injured unintentionally by a crystal alien, the alien duplicates Jack's form and out of fear of retribution tries to heal Jack. He returns to Earth in place of Jack, while Jack is still unconscious on the planet, to find the one thing he feels will accomplish this; the one thing that cannot be found again. Meanwhile, the 'real' Jack has returned and is trying to convince SGC that he is who he says he is.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: With the Stargate program in danger of being terminated, the team is under pressure to find new technology. Visiting a planet where they believe there is a creature that possesses the secret of invisibility, they encounter Apophis and his guards. Both sides receiving mortal wounds, they are aided by the planet's inhabitants, the Nox, a gentle people with a very large secret.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: SG-1 discovers a race of attractive people who age extremely rapidly. The situation becomes personal when O'Neill begins to suffer from the same accelerated aging, and must live out the rest of his life on the planet.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: SG-1 goes to the planet Cimmeria in search of allies against the Goa'uld. Upon arrival, Jack and Teal'c are trapped in a labyrinth, where the only exit is through Thor's Hammer, a device to destroy Goa'uld, but preserve the host. Daniel Jackson must destroy the device that could someday have saved his wife in order to free his friends.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Daniel Jackson discovers that the Stargate was activated in 1945 and a young professor went through, never to return. Together with his still living fiancée, the SG1 team discover the now aged professor, naked and trapped in a decaying fortress, containing the secrets of an Ancient alliance. Will they be able to rescue him and escape to earth in time?

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Teal'c returns to Chulak to stop his people from implanting his son with a larval Goa'uld.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: O'Neill, Carter and Teal'c return through the Stargate shell-shocked and distressed to announce that Daniel Jackson was killed. But Jackson was captured by the last survivor of an aquatic race, who planted false memories in the other members of SG1. Can the team remember and return to rescue Daniel?

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: The Goa'uld Hathor (one of the mothers of all Goa'uld), brainwashes the men of the SGC with pheromones, and nearly makes Jack a host to a larva conceived with Daniel. She flees after the unaffected women of Stargate Command retake the facility.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: A mysterious affliction wipes out the entire population of a planet, plus an SG team -- except for one young girl. Carter befriends her, but learns that she is being used by the Goa'uld.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: SG-1 travel to a planet where Teal'c is recognised as once having been head Jaffa to Apophis. He is arrested for the murder of a villager and put on trial for his life. Then other Jaffa attack the village. Will SG-1 be able to save Teal'c and prove that he has changed allegiance?

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: The SG-1 team rescues a group of advanced humans from a planet near destruction, and must find a new home for the refugees before the Pentagon gets their hands on them.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Following a Stargate technical defect, O'Neill and Carter are stranded next to a Stargate in a cave on an icy wasteland. O'Neill is severely wounded and both are freezing. Will the SGC find and rescue them in time?

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: After arriving on P3X-989, the team are rendered unconscious. When they wake, they return to Earth, but soon find out that they are androids. The team must return to the planet to find out what happened to their real bodies.

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Rick unknowingly causes a group of survivors to be trapped by walkers. The group dynamic devolves from accusations to violence, as Rick must confront an enemy far more dangerous than the undead.

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: After returning to the camp with the department store survivors and an emotional reunion with his wife and son, Rick decides to go against Shane's advice and go back to Atlanta for Merle Dixon and his dropped bag of guns accompanied by Merle's younger brother, Darryl Dixon, as well as Glenn and T-Dog.

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Rick, Glenn, T-Dog, and Daryl move on after their grisly discovery to retrieve the guns when they encounter a group of scavengers who narrowly beat them to the catch. However, Glenn is kidnapped in the scuffle, which leads to a tense stand-off and a surprising discovery. Meanwhile, back at camp, the group has to handle a serious situation when Jim begins acting unhinged.

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Everyone deals with the fallout of the walker attack. Andrea has to come to grips with the fate of her sister, while Jim is found out to have been bitten. With the camp clearly no longer safe, a decision must made about the direction they are to take.

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Rick and the group are allowed into the CDC by a strange doctor, but all is not what it seems in their newfound haven.

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Rick and the group leave Atlanta and encounter a threat on the highway the likes of which they've never seen. Elsewhere, the group search for a missing person.

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Rick discovers a possible safe haven. Shane must go on a dangerous mission to get needed medical supplies.

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: The group desperately awaits Shane's return. Shane finds himself trapped in a school, surrounded by the undead. Daryl and Andrea search for someone in the woods.

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Shane makes a deadly sacrifice which leads to unusual behavior and self-distancing. The rest of the group tries to hang on somewhere between living to die and dying to live.

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Concerned for their safety, the group makes a plan – one that Hershel disagrees with adamantly. He makes it clear the group is fine to stay for now, but not indefinitely.

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Secrets are told and secrets are revealed. Hershel refuses to acknowledge the world’s new reality. Andrea comes out of her shell. Everyone is becoming more and more aware that “everything is food for something.”

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Hershel sets a deadline. All secrets are out in the open. Glenn stands up for himself and Shane takes charge.

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Rick and the others try to restore order in the aftermath of a terrible discovery. Hershel takes up an old habit and disappears, Rick and Glenn must follow him into town.

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Rick, Hershel and Glen are trapped and fight to survive; Shane finds Lori in danger.

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Rick and Shane are in conflict over the fate of an outsider; Andrea helps Hershel's daughter face a crucial decision.

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Rick sides with Shane causing Dale to worry that the group is losing its humanity; Carl's actions have unintended consequences.

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Someone dangerous may be loose near the farm; Rick, Shane, Daryl and Glenn keep the group safe.

programme.desc: How its made
